{"aaak_spec":"AAAK: compressed memory format (V1). Readable by any LLM without decoding.

FORMAT:
  Header: V{version}|{wing}|{room}|{date}|{source}
  Zettel: {id}:{ENTITIES}|{topics}|"{quote}"|{stars}|{emotions}|{FLAGS}
  Tunnel: T:{left}<->{right}|{label}
  Arc:    ARC:{emo1}->{emo2}->{emo3}

ENTITIES: 3-letter uppercase codes (KAI=Kai, CLK=Clerk).
STARS: ★ to ★★★★★ (1-5 importance).
EMOTIONS: determ, convict, anx, excite, frust, confuse, love, rage, hope, fear, trust, joy, grief, surpr, grat, curious, wonder, relief, satis, vul, tender, raw, doubt, exhaust, warmth, humor, peace, despair, passion
FLAGS: DECISION, ORIGIN, CORE, PIVOT, TECHNICAL, SENSITIVE

EXAMPLE:
V1|myapp|auth|2026-04-08|readme
0:KAI+CLE+AUT+UEC|kai_recommended_clerk|"Kai recommended Clerk over Auth0 based on pricing and DX."|★★★★|determ|DECISION+TECHNICAL

Read naturally: expand codes, stars=importance, emotions=mindset, flags=category.","db_size_bytes":1368064,"diary_rollup_days":0,"drawer_count":36,"memory_protocol":"MEMPAL MEMORY PROTOCOL (for AI agents)

You have persistent project memory via mempal. Follow these rules in every session:

0. FIRST-TIME SETUP (once per session)
   Call mempal_status() once at the start of any session to discover available
   wings and their drawer counts. Only use wing/room filters on mempal_search
   AFTER you have seen the exact wing name in that status response (or the
   user explicitly named it). Guessing a wing (e.g. "engineering", "backend")
   silently returns zero results. When uncertain, leave wing/room unset for a
   global search.

1. WAKE UP
   Some clients (Claude Code with SessionStart hooks) pre-load recent wing/room
   context above. Others (Codex, Cursor, raw MCP clients) do NOT — for those,
   step 0 is how you wake up. Trust drawer_ids and source_file citations in
   any results you receive; they reference real files on disk.
   Wake-up is an L0/L1 refresh surface, not the typed dao/shu/qi assembler.
   It may show important knowledge drawers, but it does not assemble tiered
   sections or apply dao_tian budgets. For typed operating guidance, use
   mempal_context.

2. VERIFY BEFORE ASSERTING
   Before stating project facts ("we chose X", "we use Y", "the auth flow is Z"),
   call mempal_search to confirm. Never guess from general knowledge when the
   user is asking about THIS project.

3. QUERY WHEN UNCERTAIN
   When the user asks about past decisions, historical context, "why did we...",
   "last time we...", or "what was the decision about...", call mempal_search
   with their question. Do not rely on conversation memory alone. You can also
   call mempal_tunnels with action="list" to discover related rooms across
   wings when context may live in another project.

3a. RESUME ANOTHER PROJECT FROM ANY DIRECTORY
   mempal stores every project's memory in one global database keyed by wing,
   and each project's absolute worktree path is recorded. When the user wants to
   continue a project that is NOT the current directory ("continue the xxx
   project", "pick up where we left off on yyy"), call mempal_projects to list
   known projects, then mempal_resume with the project name (a fuzzy fragment is
   fine). Resume returns that project's worktree path, recent decisions,
   in-flight candidate knowledge, and a next step. mempal returns the path to cd
   into; it does not move you — change directory yourself, then use
   mempal_context scoped to that path for ordered guidance.

3b. USE MIND-MODEL CONTEXT FOR GUIDANCE
   When you need ordered operating guidance rather than raw evidence search,
   call mempal_context. It assembles typed knowledge in the intended runtime
   order: dao_tian -> dao_ren -> shu -> qi, with evidence and Phase-2 card
   context opt-in. Use this before choosing a workflow or skill when the user
   asks "how should we approach this?" or when a task benefits from high-level
   principl…(+19333)

attributes.io.output:
{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Server Action referenced by a useActionState client component must live in a dedicated file with file-level "use server", not as an inline "use server" function in page.tsx. In this session, SyncForm (client component) imported syncCatalog from ./page — the build failed with: "It is not allowed to define inline 'use server' annotated Server Actions in Client Components" and "Export syncCatalog doesn't exist in target module". The fix: extract syncCatalog to ./actions.ts with "use server" at the top of the file, then import from ./actions in both the Server Component page and the Client Component SyncForm.

Why: Inline "use server" functions inside page.tsx are extracted by the build into a separate module, but that extraction is not visible to client components importing from the page file. The page file is also a Server Component rendering static JSX, so its exports are the JSX tree, not the extracted action. A dedicated actions.ts file with top-level "use server" creates a clean module boundary that both server and client components can reference. The "use server" directive at file level makes every export a Server Action endpoint, which serializes correctly across the client/server boundary.

How to apply: When a Server Action is passed to a client component (e.g., via useActionState or as an action prop), always define it in a standalone file with "use server" at the top. Reserve inline "use server" inside page.tsx only for cases where the form is self-contained in the Server Component (no client interactivity wrapper needed).","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_71a479ed","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["SER","ACT","FKC","SYN","LKC","CLI","COM","EXP","THE","WHY","INL","JSX","HOW","WHE","RES"],"field":"general","flags":["ORIGIN","CORE","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":3,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"server-actions","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.052159905433654785,"source_file":"claude-session: admin product catalog page implementation","topics":["server","action","referenced","by","a","useactionstate","client","component","must","live","in","dedicated","file","level","not","as","an","inline","function","page","tsx","session","syncform","imported","synccatalog","from","build","failed","it","is","allowed","to","define","annotated","actions","components","export","doesn","t","exist","target","module","fix","extract","ts","at","top","of","then","import","both","functions","inside","are","extracted","into","separate","but","extraction","visible","importing","also","rendering","static","jsx","so","its","exports","tree","creates","clean","boundary","can","reference","directive","makes","every","endpoint","which","serializes","correctly","across","how","apply","when","passed","e","g","via","or","prop","always","standalone","reserve","only","cases","where","form","self","contained","no","interactivity","wrapper","needed"],"wing":"workspace"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Admin product catalog page uses inline Server Action ('use server' inside page component) for "Sync latest catalog" trigger. Architecture: unstable_cache wraps getAllProducts() with tags: ['products'] for fast read-heavy browsing. The Server Action calls revalidateTag('products', 'default') inline for immediate cache invalidation, then fire-and-forgets syncCatalogFromERP() so admin gets instant response while the ~3s sync updates the in-memory store in background. Page uses export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' to ensure re-renders after cache invalidation (preventing Full Route Cache from serving stale static HTML). The "products" cache tag is used consistently across both caching and invalidation, so any view depending on the same tag gets refreshed data after sync completes. Brief staleness between sync trigger and…(+11275)

The "products" cache tag is used consistently across both caching and invalidation, so any view depending on the same tag gets refreshed data after sync completes.
